findings.title: Stored XSS: combobox option/value text rendered unescaped into innerHTML sink
findings.description: ComboboxOption renders `{props.children ?? props.itemLabel ?? props.itemValue}` (line 248) and ComboboxValue renders `{comboboxValueText(props)}` (line 277). `comboboxValueText` (packages/headless-ui/src/primitives/combobox.ts:143-148) returns raw item data (`selected.label ?? selected.textValue ?? selected.value`, or the current `value`/`placeholder`). These scalar DATA props are placed in child position, and the server JSX runtime does not escape child text (`return String(children)`, packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts:83). The resulting HTML is delivered in the initial document and in wire fragments applied client-side via `insertAdjacentHTML`/`innerHTML` (packages/runtime/src/response-fragment-apply.ts:58,62), matching threat-model stored-XSS #3. Combobox item lists are typically DB-sourced (the gallery demo binds them to people's names); an attacker controlling a label/value (e.g. a chosen display name or product title) injects executing markup like `<svg onload=...>` into every viewer's page. The bug is an oversight, not a contract: `itemValue` is emitted as an auto-escaped attribute (`value={attrs.value}`, line 246) AND as an unescaped child, so the caller cannot pre-escape it without corrupting the attribute — the component must escape the child and does not. Note the option element here is a `<div role=option>` (line 237), so injected markup renders directly (unlike a native `<option>`).
findings.recommendation: Escape DB/user-derived text in child position. Preferred: fix renderJsxChildren in packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts to HTML-escape string children with an explicit raw-HTML opt-out for composition. Otherwise, in this component wrap data text with escapeHtml (from @jiso/server): `{props.children ?? escapeHtml(props.itemLabel ?? props.itemValue)}` and `{escapeHtml(comboboxValueText(props))}`. Resolve the framework's tracked text-escaping open question to fix this class of bug everywhere.
